museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: January 2016: The Witness turns January into a puzzle island.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: The Witness
items.body: A puzzle island becomes a notebook, a headache and a quiet obsession.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for January 26, 2016.

items.title: Firewatch
items.body: A walking-sim mystery turns solitude and radio chatter into a shared mood.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for February 9, 2016.

items.title: Stardew Valley
items.body: A solo-made farming game becomes a comfort ritual for years.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for February 26, 2016.

items.title: Dark Souls III
items.body: FromSoftware closes a cycle of ash, punishment and community archaeology.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for April 12, 2016 internationally.

items.title: Uncharted 4: A Thief's End
items.body: Naughty Dog gives PS4 one of its defining cinematic showcases.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 10, 2016.

items.title: Doom
items.body: id Software turns an old name into speed, metal and forward momentum.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 13, 2016.

items.title: Overwatch
items.body: Blizzard makes hero composition, counters and payload chatter mainstream.
items.sourceNote: PlayStation Blog lists May 24, 2016.

items.title: Inside
items.body: Playdead makes silence, bodies and dread feel beautifully exact.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for late June 2016.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: Xbox One S launches
items.body: Microsoft's slimmer console adds 4K video and HDR into a mid-generation refresh conversation.
items.sourceNote: Xbox Wire lists August 2, 2016.

items.title: PlayStation VR launches
items.body: Sony brings console VR to the high street, complete with demo pods, cables and cautious wonder.
items.sourceNote: PlayStation Blog lists October 13, 2016.

items.title: PS4 Pro launches
items.body: Sony introduces a more powerful PS4 and makes 4K/HDR part of console marketing.
items.sourceNote: PlayStation Blog lists November 10, 2016.

items.title: Nintendo reveals Switch
items.body: Nintendo's hybrid-console trailer changes NX from mystery codename to visible object.
items.sourceNote: Nintendo Switch references list October 20, 2016 reveal.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Streams and servers
items.body: Pokemon Go briefly turns online maps into outdoor gatherings, town-centre loops and battery-pack rituals.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Services and stores
items.body: Overwatch makes hero picks, counters, Play of the Game clips and seasonal events part of everyday online speech.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Forums and feeds
items.body: No Man's Sky becomes a case study in hype, refunds, forums and the long life of post-launch repair.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: Discord, Twitch and Reddit increasingly define the first public draft of a game's reputation.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like the generation was being stretched. Not replaced, exactly, but upgraded, accessorised and questioned.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: VR was both magical and faintly awkward: shop demos, headset hair, cables, motion sickness stories and one person playing while everyone else watched.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: Pokemon Go made gaming public in a way that felt almost surreal, especially in parks and high streets where players recognised each other without speaking.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: By October, the Switch trailer gave Nintendo fans something they had badly needed: not proof yet, but a shape for hope.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
